# T3DS - TanStack Dokploy Directus DaisyUI Starter

> A pnpm workspace monorepo with TanStack Router, Query, Directus CMS, and DaisyUI components

## Project Structure

- packages/app: Main TanStack React application (Vite, React 19, TanStack Router & Query, Tailwind CSS, DaisyUI)
- packages/directus: Directus SDK configuration and types (Node.js package)
- packages/dokploy: Dokploy deployment configuration

## Core Rules

- Always use TypeScript (never JavaScript)
- Use pnpm exclusively for package management
- Single quotes, semicolons, 2-space indentation, Unix line endings
- Strict type checking, avoid `any` types
- Follow ESLint and Prettier configurations

## Module System

- packages/directus (Node.js): Use .js extensions in relative imports due to moduleResolution: "NodeNext"
- packages/app (Vite): No extensions in imports
- Workspace imports: @t3ds/directus
- App path aliases: @/* for packages/app/src/*
- IMPORTANT: Always use static imports for @/utils/directus in server handlers - dynamic imports prevent NITRO_DIRECTUS_URL from being read correctly
- IMPORTANT: Never use top-level static imports from @tanstack/react-start/server in files reachable from the client route tree. Use dynamic `import('@tanstack/react-start/server')` inside createServerFn handlers instead. TanStack Start's import-protection plugin blocks both direct server imports and *.server.* file patterns from client code.
- For authenticated Directus requests in server functions, use `directusRequest(command, true)` from @/utils/directus
  - Example: `const user = await directusRequest(readMe(), true)`
  - Returns `null` if no token is available
  - For public collections, use `directusRequest(command)` (auth defaults to false)

## Commands

- pnpm install: Install all dependencies
- pnpm dev: Run development server (port 3000)
- pnpm build: Build for production
- pnpm lint: Lint all packages
- pnpm check: Format and fix linting issues
- pnpm test: Run tests
- pnpm --filter @t3ds/app <cmd>: Run app-specific command
- pnpm --filter @t3ds/directus init: Initialize Directus collections

## Stack

- React 19
- TanStack Router (file-based routing in packages/app/src/routes)
- TanStack Query for data fetching
- Tailwind CSS 4 (only in packages/app)
- DaisyUI 5 for UI components
- Vitest for testing
- TypeScript 5.9.3
- Directus SDK 21.2.2
- Intlayer 8.7.0 (internationalization)

## Documentation

- README.md: Getting started and main documentation
- docs/directus/README.md: Directus setup and configuration
- docs/dokploy_directus/dokploy_directus.md: Production deployment guide
- .github/AGENT_GUIDELINES.md: Comprehensive AI agent guidelines

## File Structure

- packages/app/src/routes/: File-based routing with co-located components and data
  - Each route is a folder with index.tsx (route definition), [feature].page.tsx (UI component), and [feature].data.ts (server functions)
  - Route folders: login/, landing/, demo/songs/, _authenticated/user/
- packages/app/src/components/: Reusable React components
- packages/app/src/components/daisyui/: DaisyUI component wrappers (Button, Input, Card, Navigation)
- packages/app/src/contents/: Intlayer content files for translations
- packages/app/src/hooks/: Custom React hooks
- packages/app/src/utils/: Utility functions (cookie, directus, head)
  - directus.ts: Directus client (getClient), server functions for auth (isAuthenticated, getCookieToken, setCookieToken) using dynamic server imports
- packages/directus/src/index.ts: Directus collections and configuration
- packages/directus/src/types.ts: TypeScript type definitions
- packages/directus/src/init.ts: Database migration script
- packages/directus/src/.env.local: Environment configuration (tracked in git)

## Environment Variables

- DIRECTUS_STATIC_TOKEN: Required for database migrations (in packages/directus/src/.env.local)
- Note: .env.local is tracked in git for the directus package (exception to typical patterns)

## DaisyUI Components

- Button: Supports variant (primary, secondary, accent, neutral), size, wide, block props
- Input: Supports label prop for integrated label/input component
- Card: Supports title, actions, size props
- Navigation: Navbar component with brand and menu items

## Internationalization

- Use getIntlayer with locale parameter from useParams for translations
- Content files in packages/app/src/contents/ (app.content.ts, landing.content.ts, user.content.ts, index.content.ts)
- Supports English (en) and German (de)

## Landing Page Flow

- First-time visitors are redirected to /landing page
- Landing cookie is set after visiting landing page (expires in 1 year)
- Returning visitors go directly to home page
- Cookie management via useCookie hook

## Authentication

- Authentication uses Directus SDK with httpOnly cookie-based token storage
- Login: POST handler in login/index.tsx calls Directus login, stores tokens via setCookieToken
- Route protection: _authenticated.tsx layout route uses beforeLoad + isAuthenticated server function
- isAuthenticated checks session cookie, refreshes token if expiring soon, validates with readMe()
- Token storage: three httpOnly cookies (session token, refresh token, expiry timestamp)
- For authenticated API calls in server functions: use `directusRequest(command, auth)` pattern
  - Example: `const user = await directusRequest(readMe(), true)`
  - Example: `const items = (await directusRequest(readItems('private', {}), true)) ?? []`
  - Example public: `const songs = (await directusRequest(readItems('songs', {}))) ?? []`
  - Returns `null` if no session token available (auth=true) 
- Public collections (e.g. songs) use `getClient()` without token
- Private collections require withToken — ensure Directus role has read access

## Best Practices

- Use feature-based file structure: each route is a self-contained folder
- Route folders contain: index.tsx (route definition), [feature].page.tsx (UI component), [feature].data.ts (server functions)
- Files with .page.tsx, .data.ts, or .types.ts extensions are automatically excluded from route generation
- Keep business logic separate from UI components
- Leverage TypeScript's type system fully
- Extract common logic into utilities or hooks
- Use React.memo, useMemo, useCallback appropriately
- Ensure UI components are accessible (ARIA labels, keyboard navigation)
- Handle errors gracefully with try-catch or error boundaries
- Add JSDoc comments for complex functions and types
- Use DaisyUI semantic color names (base-*, primary, secondary, etc.) instead of Tailwind color classes
